senses.definition: a close friend or companion, often used in a more negative or informal context

examples.x: He's always with his cronies, making shady deals.
They've been business cronies for years, and no one trusts them.
She's hanging out with her old cronies at the bar tonight.
Those two are inseparable, always getting into trouble together, like a couple of cronies.
After years of being cronies, they started to turn against each other.

senses.definition: a strong and natural liking or sympathy toward someone or something

examples.x: She felt an instant affinity with the new student, recognizing a shared love for literature.
His affinity for animals led him to volunteer at the local shelter every weekend.
Despite their different backgrounds, they formed a strong affinity through their shared experiences.
The artist had an affinity for vibrant colors, which was evident in her bold and expressive paintings.
He felt a deep affinity for nature, finding solace and inspiration in the beauty of the outdoors.

senses.definition: a group of people who have the same profession

examples.x: The fraternity of artists gathered every month to critique each other's work and share new techniques.
She joined the fraternity of scientists dedicated to finding solutions to environmental issues.
The fraternity of writers supported each other through workshops and writing retreats.
As a member of the fraternity of engineers, he enjoyed networking with other professionals in his field.
The fraternity of musicians collaborated on projects and performed together at local venues.

senses.definition: pleasant, friendly, and peaceful relations between individuals or nations

examples.x: The two neighboring countries signed a treaty to promote amity and cooperation between their citizens.
Her warm smile and kind words fostered a sense of amity among her coworkers.
The festival was a celebration of amity, bringing together people from diverse backgrounds to enjoy the festivities.
Their decades-long amity was a testament to their mutual respect and understanding.
The community center was established to encourage amity and collaboration among local residents.

senses.definition: an opponent or enemy

examples.x: The hero faced his greatest foe in the final battle, determined to protect his kingdom.
In the political arena, she found herself with many foes who opposed her policies.
Despite their differences, they realized that a common foe could unite them for a greater cause.
He trained tirelessly to outsmart his formidable foe in the upcoming chess tournament.
The company viewed the new competitor as a formidable foe in the market.

senses.definition: absence of agreement or friendliness between people with different opinions

examples.x: There was noticeable friction between the team members after the disagreement over the project's direction.
The new policies introduced by management caused friction among the employees.
Political debates often lead to friction among friends with differing views.
The friction between the two departments hindered their ability to collaborate effectively.
The teacher tried to mediate the friction between the students to restore a harmonious classroom environment.

senses.definition: a violent argument between two groups in which members of each side make attempts to murder the members of the opposing side in retaliation for things that occurred in the past

examples.x: The families had been embroiled in a vendetta for generations, each seeking revenge for past wrongs.
The vendetta between the rival gangs resulted in numerous violent clashes and a high death toll.
Despite efforts at peace, the vendetta continued to fuel hatred and bloodshed in the community.
The movie depicted a tragic vendetta, where characters were driven by a cycle of vengeance and retribution.
Authorities struggled to intervene in the vendetta, as deep-seated grudges made reconciliation nearly impossible.

senses.definition: an end to a friendly relationship between people or organizations caused by a serious disagreement

examples.x: The rift between the two longtime friends started over a minor disagreement but quickly escalated into a full-blown feud.
The scandal caused a significant rift within the organization, leading to resignations and infighting.
Efforts to heal the rift between the two countries were unsuccessful, and diplomatic relations remained strained.
The family's rift began when the inheritance was disputed, creating lasting divisions among siblings.
The rift in their relationship became apparent when they stopped communicating altogether.

senses.definition: the passing down of traits from one generation to another within a family

examples.x: She inherited her musical talent from her mother, continuing the lineage of musicians in their family.
The renowned chef was proud to carry on the culinary lineage of their family, using traditional recipes passed down through generations.
She was proud of her royal lineage, tracing her ancestry back to ancient kings and queens.
The scientist studied the genetic lineage of the endangered species to understand its evolutionary history.
The family's lineage included a long line of doctors, each generation contributing to the medical field.

senses.definition: a family in which the parents live with the children from their own relationship along with the children from previous ones

examples.x: Holidays were a joyful occasion for their blended family, with children from both previous marriages coming together to celebrate.
Navigating the dynamics of a blended family can be challenging, but open communication helped them build strong relationships.
The blended family took a vacation to bond and create new memories as a united household.
Each member of the blended family brought their own traditions, enriching their shared cultural experiences.
It took time and patience, but their blended family eventually found harmony and mutual respect.

senses.definition: the state of being biologically related to someone

examples.x: The royal family's strict rules on marriage were based on maintaining consanguinity to preserve their bloodline.
The genetic study revealed a high degree of consanguinity among the isolated population, indicating close familial relationships.
Laws regarding inheritance often take consanguinity into account to determine rightful heirs.
The consanguinity between the cousins was evident, as they shared many physical and personality traits.
In some cultures, consanguinity plays a significant role in marriage arrangements, ensuring that familial ties remain strong.

senses.definition: the recorded ancestry or lineage of individuals, typically in the context of their descendants tracing back to a common ancestor

examples.x: Her family has a long and respected pedigree in the community.
He comes from a pedigree of doctors, with several generations practicing medicine.
In politics, his pedigree is well-regarded, coming from a family of distinguished politicians.
Coming from a pedigree of athletes, he excels in various sports.

senses.definition: one or all the descendants of an ancestor

examples.x: The scientist was excited to study the progeny of the genetically modified plants to see if the desired traits were passed on.
The renowned artist took great pride in his progeny, many of whom followed in his footsteps to pursue creative careers.
The farmer's progeny were expected to take over the family business and continue the agricultural tradition.
His progeny showed a remarkable talent for music, much like their father and grandfather before them.
The queen's progeny included several princes and princesses, each destined to play a significant role in the kingdom's future.

senses.definition: a woman who agrees to carry and take the responsibility of another couple's child

examples.x: After years of struggling with infertility, they decided to seek the help of a surrogate mother to carry their child.
The surrogate mother formed a close bond with the couple, sharing updates and experiences throughout the pregnancy.
Choosing a surrogate mother was a deeply personal decision that required careful consideration and trust.
The surrogate mother gave birth to healthy twins, bringing immense joy to the intended parents.
The laws surrounding the rights of a surrogate mother vary significantly from one country to another.

senses.definition: a person from whom one inherits DNA and is directly responsible for their birth

examples.x: The child was curious about their biological parent and wanted to learn more about their family history.
The biological parent played an important role in the child's life, providing love and support as they grew up.
She decided to reconnect with her biological parents later in life, eager to learn more about her roots.
The court granted visitation rights to the biological parent, allowing them to maintain a relationship with their child.

senses.definition: a person from whom other offsprings are descended

examples.x: The ancient civilization's progenitors laid the foundation for the cultural traditions that endured for centuries.
The scientist's groundbreaking research paved the way for future discoveries, establishing him as a progenitor in his field.
The company's founders were the progenitors of innovation, revolutionizing the industry with their groundbreaking ideas.
The artist's unique style became the progenitor of a new artistic movement that swept through the art world.
In biology, the study of DNA reveals clues about the genetic makeup passed down from progenitors to descendants.

senses.definition: the act of running away with one's lover to get married without the consent of parents

examples.x: Their spontaneous elopement to Las Vegas was met with excitement and disbelief by their friends.
Despite objections from their parents, they were determined to go ahead with their elopement and start a new life together.

senses.definition: the period of time when two people are getting to know each other romantically with the intention of getting married

examples.x: Their courtship was marked by romantic gestures and long walks in the park.
Courtship rituals vary across cultures, but they often involve displays of affection and mutual interest.
He wrote her love letters during their courtship, expressing his feelings with eloquence and sincerity.
The couple's courtship lasted for several years before they finally decided to get married.
In the animal kingdom, courtship behaviors can be elaborate and serve to attract a mate for reproduction.

senses.definition: sexual intercourse involving a married person and someone other than their spouse

examples.x: Despite their vows of fidelity, she discovered evidence of her husband's adultery with a coworker.
In the eyes of the law, adultery can be grounds for divorce in many jurisdictions.
The scandalous affair rocked the small town, revealing a web of adultery and deceit.
Adultery has been a common theme in literature and drama, often serving as a catalyst for complex plotlines and character development.
Despite the temptation, she remained committed to her marriage vows and chose to confront her husband about his suspected adultery.

senses.definition: a temporary and intense feeling of romantic or idealized attraction toward someone, often based on superficial qualities and lacking a deep emotional connection

examples.x: Her infatuation with the new coworker was evident to everyone in the office, despite her attempts to hide it.
The teenager's infatuation with the pop star led her to plaster her room with posters and attend every concert.
He mistook his infatuation with her beauty for genuine love, only to realize later that it was fleeting and superficial.
Infatuation often clouds judgment and leads to impulsive decisions, especially in matters of the heart.
Their whirlwind romance was fueled by infatuation rather than genuine compatibility, and it quickly fizzled out.

senses.definition: to reject or deny any association or relationship with someone or something

examples.x: He chose to disown his former beliefs after learning more about the issue.
The politician disowned her previous statements when they caused public outrage.
The artist disowned his earlier works, feeling they no longer represented him.
She disowned the nickname her friends gave her, preferring her real name.
He disowned his membership in the organization due to their changing values.

senses.definition: to put an end to an argument with someone in order to make peace with them

examples.x: They had a huge fight but managed to patch up before the day ended.
He made an effort to patch up with his old friend after realizing the misunderstanding.
Their relationship was strained, but a heartfelt conversation helped them patch up.

senses.definition: to revive or renew something, such as a relationship or interest, that has faded

examples.x: After years apart, they managed to rekindle their childhood friendship.
She listened to her favorite song to rekindle her passion for music.
Attending a concert rekindled her enthusiasm for live music.
Attending a class reunion rekindled friendships from years ago.
Spending time with her siblings rekindled the bond they shared growing up.

senses.definition: to provoke and anger someone so much that they start to hate and oppose one

examples.x: His constant criticism of her work antagonized her to the point of seeking employment elsewhere.
The politician's controversial remarks antagonized many voters.
The company's decision to cut benefits antagonized its employees.
Her refusal to compromise antagonized her colleagues.
The aggressive tone of the letter antagonized the recipient.

senses.definition: to gradually become less close or connected, often due to a lack of shared interests or diverging paths

examples.x: Over the years, the friends started to drift apart as their lives took different paths.
Despite their strong bond in college, the siblings began to drift apart after moving to separate cities.
The couple realized they were drifting apart due to conflicting priorities and interests.
In the fast-paced world of business, colleagues often drift apart as they pursue individual career goals.
As childhood friends grow older, they may naturally drift apart as new responsibilities and commitments arise.

senses.definition: to have a lasting and heated argument with someone

examples.x: The families continued to feud over a disputed piece of land, passing down the animosity through generations.
Despite being friends once, they started to feud after a misunderstanding, causing a rift in their relationship.
The rival gangs feuded over control of the neighborhood for years.
The neighboring kingdoms feuded for decades over trade routes and resources.
The siblings feuded over their inheritance after the parents passed away.

senses.definition: to betray one's partner by secretly having an affair with someone else at the same time
